basis: Approx. 1 ransomware event per 3–4 years; small- to mid-market fintech firm without mature SOC. Derived from DBIR financial and insurance sector system intrusion pattern prevalence and ransomware action frequency (23.0% of breaches across all industries; financial sector among top patterns).
source_full: Verizon Business. 2024 Data Breach Investigations Report. Verizon Communications Inc., 2024.
notes: Base case. Sensitivity sweep uses λ ∈ {0.10 0.30 0.50}.

basis: 42.6% of $6.08M financial services average breach cost. IBM decomposition attributes ~42.6% of total cost to detection escalation notification and response activities (primary loss).
source_short: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024
source_full: IBM Security and Ponemon Institute. Cost of a Data Breach Report 2024. IBM Corporation, 2024.
notes: Used as log-normal median anchor. Back-solve: mu = log(P50).

basis: 2.5× median. Conservative multiplier reflecting moderate operational tail for firms with partial IR capability. Documented assumption — not derived from IBM data.
source_short: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024
source_full: IBM Security and Ponemon Institute. Cost of a Data Breach Report 2024. IBM Corporation, 2024.
notes: Sensitivity parameter. Back-solve: sigma = (log(P90) - log(P50)) / qnorm(0.90).

basis: 57.4% of $6.08M financial services average breach cost. IBM decomposition attributes ~57.4% of total cost to lost business post-breach customer support and regulatory fines (secondary loss).
source_short: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024
source_full: IBM Security and Ponemon Institute. Cost of a Data Breach Report 2024. IBM Corporation, 2024.
notes: Used as log-normal median anchor. Back-solve: mu = log(P50).

basis: 3.5× median. Heavier multiplier reflecting regulatory and litigation tail exposure specific to financial services firms. Documented assumption — not derived from IBM data.
source_short: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024
source_full: IBM Security and Ponemon Institute. Cost of a Data Breach Report 2024. IBM Corporation, 2024.
notes: Sensitivity parameter. Back-solve: sigma = (log(P90) - log(P50)) / qnorm(0.90).

basis: Standard for stable convergence at 95th and 99th percentile tail estimates. FAIR Institute best practice.
source_full: FAIR Institute. Factor Analysis of Information Risk (FAIR) Standard v3.0. FAIR Institute, January 2025.
notes: Increase to 500000 for publication-quality tail stability if render time permits.

basis: Reproducibility. Set once at top of simulation chunk.
notes: Change only with documented rationale to preserve reproducibility.

basis: IBM 2024 reported financial services industry average total breach cost — second highest of any industry. Used as the denominator for primary/secondary split calculation. Not a direct model parameter.

source_full: IBM Security and Ponemon Institute. Cost of a Data Breach Report 2024. IBM Corporation, 2024.

basis: IBM 2024 global average total breach cost across all industries. Used to derive primary/secondary split ratio (direct costs $2.08M / post-breach costs $2.80M). Not a direct model parameter.

source_full: IBM Security and Ponemon Institute. Cost of a Data Breach Report 2024. IBM Corporation, 2024.
